seed: vulnerability vs strength; exposure_vs_shelter; rising_signal; outside_inward; flood against spark; hard_edges; thin_vs_heavy; tiny_plus; connection_alienation | abstract variation request 483; target batch count 500
title: Core Collision
reasoning: The emotional tone of this artwork embodies the tension between vulnerability and strength, evoking feelings of both exposure and protection. I chose a palette of lime, black, electric blue, and magenta to symbolize a juxtaposition of light and darkness. The black offers a grounding contrast, while lime and electric blue create a vibrant, pulsating energy, suggesting rising signals against the backdrop of shelter. The layout, inspired by a spiral pressure, draws colors toward the center—creating a dynamic push and pull that mirrors conflict and connection. The main collision occurs in the center where the strong edges of black sharply contrast with the soft dots of lime and magenta, representing the interaction of resilience against fragility. Broken pixels are strategically placed in the perimeter, creating intentional disruptions akin to cracks in strength, contributing to the theme of vulnerability.

layout.name: spiral_pressure
layout.description: Marks coil toward or away from a small off-center point.

composition.name: center_vs_edges
composition.idea: The center is protected or attacked by the surrounding pixels.

referenceExample.title: Awe at the Edge
referenceExample.pattern: black hollow center surrounded by cold gold and star-white pressure

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
